I conclude with the words of Ezekiel, "And thou, son of man, … though briers and thorns be with thee, and thou dost dwell among scorpions: be not afraid" (Ezekiel 2:6). In our constantly changing world, may we continually cling to those things that do not change: to prayer, to faith, to saving covenants, to love of families, and to brotherhood. written within and on the backside. The technical term for this book is an opistographi, which is a scroll that has writing on both sides. Ezekiel's scroll is similarly described: "I looked, . . . and [the scroll] was written within and without: and there was written therein lamentations, and mourning, and woe" (Ezek. 2:9-10). The book in God's right hand overflows with his "revealed will,…
